verification.note: Equity-oriented funds use the same Section 112A/111A rates as direct listed equity (see capital-gains-equity.json), unchanged for FY2025-26. Section 50AA 'specified mutual fund' threshold added 2026-07-06 CA review: the operative test for FY2025-26/AY2026-27 is a fund investing more than 65% in debt/money-market instruments (or at least 65% in units of such funds) - not the earlier, narrower '<=35% equity' formulation some older material still uses. A fund in the roughly 35-65% equity range does not automatically get deemed-short-term treatment; it may instead get equity or other-capital-asset treatment depending on its actual category. This tool cannot yet read a fund's exact portfolio composition from a statement alone, so needs_fund_type_check remains true. Cross-checked against ClearTax and TaxGuru coverage of the Section 50AA redefinition; confirm the exact fund category with the AMC's factsheet before relying on this for a specific fund.

source_refs: capital-gains-equity.json (Section 112A/111A rates used for equity-oriented funds)

specified_funds_section_50aa.threshold_note: A fund investing more than 65% in debt/money-market instruments (or at least 65% in units of such funds) is a 'specified mutual fund' under Section 50AA: deemed short-term, taxed at slab rate, no indexation, regardless of actual holding period. A fund below that threshold is not automatically caught by 50AA and may instead get equity-oriented or other-capital-asset treatment.
